title: Implement fail-closed Work Item lifecycle closure

sources.reason: Defines one Work Item, one branch, one PR and repository-role-aware branch bases.
Defines the archived Contract/Summary and no-active Cockpit status boundary.
Defines the Work Item lifecycle and generated status checks.

riskAssessment.reason: Closure performs destructive local and remote branch cleanup, so every precondition and postcondition must fail closed.

executionDecision.reason: User approved the lifecycle closure design and the new independent Work Item.

preReviewWarnings: Review destructive cleanup ordering, exact PR/branch mapping, fast-forward-only synchronization, and fail-closed behavior.

checkpointPolicy.reason: Record at least one checkpoint before finishing to reduce mid-task drift.

acceptance: make ai-close-work-item TASK=<task> verifies archived Contract/Summary/Cockpit Status consistency before cleanup.
The command verifies the current branch, Work Item, and PR are one-to-one and that the PR is merged before switching or deleting branches.
The command fast-forward synchronizes the configured base branch, deletes the local branch, deletes the remote branch, and verifies clean state plus local-base equality with the remote base.
Every failed precondition or cleanup step stops without marking the Work Item closed; the remote deletion occurs only after local main/base safety is established.
Tests cover successful closure and failures for incomplete evidence, unmerged PR, branch mismatch, non-fast-forward synchronization, failed remote deletion, and dirty state.
Rules and documentation describe the lifecycle closure command, repository-role-aware base branch configuration, and the final ready-for-next-task state.
guidelines: Destructive cleanup must be fail closed and ordered to preserve local recovery capability.
Never hard-code origin/main for adopter projects; use Contract workflow metadata for remote and base branch.
Keep closure verification platform-aware without treating local output as proof of PR identity.

intent.problem: A Work Item can be archived and its PR merged while the repository still has a stale local base branch, a retained work branch, a remote branch, or an inconsistent Cockpit status. Manual cleanup can also delete a branch before confirming merge, weakening recovery.
constraints: Closure must use the Work Item's configured remote and base branch, not assume origin/main.
PR merge must be verified through the hosting platform adapter before branch deletion.
Local base synchronization must be fast-forward only.
Remote deletion must happen after local base safety and local branch deletion.
Failure must leave the Work Item unclosed and must not claim repository readiness.
intent.rationale: A deterministic, fail-closed closure command makes the repository state ready for the next Work Item and provides the safety foundation for parallel branch/PR execution.

scenarioCoverage.scenario: A completed archived Work Item with a merged matching PR closes to a clean synchronized base branch.
An unmerged or branch-mismatched PR prevents any branch cleanup and does not report closure.
A failed fast-forward or remote deletion leaves the process failed closed after preserving local base safety.

restrictedWriteApproval.reason: User explicitly approved implementation of the lifecycle closure command, tests, rules, and documentation.

rollbackNote: Revert this Work Item diff and restore related tests and docs.
